obfuscation_patterns.name: chr() chain — string building
obfuscation_patterns.reason: Multiple chr() calls concatenated — classic technique to build a string without using string literals, hiding the actual content from static analysis.
obfuscation_patterns.context: Sometimes used legitimately in code generation or serialization

obfuscation_patterns.name: XOR decode loop
obfuscation_patterns.reason: XOR operation with a constant — common in XOR-based payload decryption routines.
obfuscation_patterns.context: Rarely used legitimately; often indicates data obfuscation

obfuscation_patterns.name: Base64 decode → exec/eval
obfuscation_patterns.reason: Base64-decoded content passed directly to eval()/exec() — classic encoded payload execution pattern.
obfuscation_patterns.context: Almost never legitimate; indicates malicious payload execution

obfuscation_patterns.name: Base64 decode → compile
obfuscation_patterns.reason: Base64-decoded content passed to compile() — obfuscated code compiled and likely executed.
obfuscation_patterns.context: Rarely legitimate; indicates runtime code generation from encoded data

obfuscation_patterns.name: zlib decompress + base64 decode chain
obfuscation_patterns.reason: zlib decompression chained with base64 decoding — signature of a packed/compressed payload being unpacked at runtime.
obfuscation_patterns.context: Sometimes used in legitimate compression, but suspicious with eval/exec

obfuscation_patterns.name: XOR decode with chr() and join
obfuscation_patterns.reason: XOR-based string decoding using chr(c ^ key) in a generator expression — common technique to decode hidden strings at runtime.
obfuscation_patterns.context: Rarely used legitimately; often indicates data obfuscation or payload decryption

obfuscation_patterns.name: eval/exec with decode chain
obfuscation_patterns.reason: eval()/exec() called on decoded/decompressed content — encoded payload being unpacked and executed.
obfuscation_patterns.context: Almost never legitimate; indicates malicious payload execution

obfuscation_patterns.name: Dynamic __import__() call
obfuscation_patterns.reason: __import__() called with a non-literal argument — module name is determined at runtime to evade static import analysis.
obfuscation_patterns.context: Sometimes used in legitimate plugin systems or dynamic loading

obfuscation_patterns.name: String concat to build module name
obfuscation_patterns.reason: Module name built by string concatenation before __import__ — hides which module is being imported.
obfuscation_patterns.context: Rarely legitimate; often indicates evasion of static analysis

obfuscation_patterns.name: exec(compile()) — runtime code compilation
obfuscation_patterns.reason: exec(compile(...)) — code is compiled and executed at runtime, bypassing static analysis of what runs.
obfuscation_patterns.context: Almost never legitimate; indicates advanced evasion techniques

obfuscation_patterns.name: Hex escape sequences in string
obfuscation_patterns.reason: Four or more consecutive hex escape sequences — commonly used to encode hidden strings or shellcode.
obfuscation_patterns.context: Often used in legitimate code for special characters or Unicode

obfuscation_patterns.name: Octal escape sequences in string
obfuscation_patterns.reason: Multiple octal escape sequences — alternate encoding method to hide string content from static inspection.
obfuscation_patterns.context: Often used in legitimate code for character encoding

obfuscation_patterns.name: Unicode escape sequences in string
obfuscation_patterns.reason: Multiple Unicode escape sequences — can be used to hide keywords or strings from pattern matching.
obfuscation_patterns.context: Very common in legitimate internationalization and string encoding

obfuscation_patterns.name: getattr() used to call function dynamically
obfuscation_patterns.reason: getattr() called with a non-literal attribute name — function to call is determined at runtime, hiding behavior.
obfuscation_patterns.context: Common in legitimate dynamic dispatch patterns in frameworks

obfuscation_patterns.name: Lambda chain obfuscation
obfuscation_patterns.reason: Deeply nested lambda expressions — can be used to obfuscate logic and evade simple pattern matching.
obfuscation_patterns.context: Sometimes used in functional programming, but excessive nesting is suspicious

obfuscation_patterns.name: codecs.decode / rot13 obfuscation
obfuscation_patterns.reason: codecs.decode with rot13 — used to hide string literals like function names or URLs from static inspection.
obfuscation_patterns.context: Rarely used legitimately; often indicates evasion of pattern matching
